claimText: Operation Olivia is an initiative that protects Olive Ridley Turtles on Odisha's Gahirmatha coast.
evidenceExcerpt: Operation Olivia shielded over 8-lakh ORT on Odisha's Gahirmatha coast.
examUse: Statement-based question on conservation operations and their target species or locations.

claimText: The IUCN conservation status of the Olive Ridley Turtle is Vulnerable.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on species conservation status.

claimText: Olive Ridley Turtles are protected under Schedule I of the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972, and Appendix I of CITES.
evidenceExcerpt: Conservation Status: WPA, 1972: Schedule I. CITES: Appendix I.
examUse: Statement-based question on legal protection of marine species.

claimText: Olive Ridley Turtles undergo a unique mass nesting phenomenon known as arribadas.
evidenceExcerpt: Undergo unique mass nesting called arribadas.
examUse: Terminology-based question or statement-based question on species behavior.

claimText: Olive Ridley Turtles are the smallest and most abundant of all sea turtles.
evidenceExcerpt: Smallest and most abundant of all sea turtles.
examUse: Statement-based question on species characteristics and comparisons.

claimText: The habitat of Olive Ridley Turtles consists of warm, tropical waters of the Pacific, Atlantic, and Indian Oceans.
evidenceExcerpt: Habitat: Warm, tropical waters of the Pacific, Atlantic, and Indian Oceans.
examUse: Statement-based question on geographical distribution and habitat.
distractorHooks: Restricted only to the Indian Ocean
